title: Calydorea venezolensis
taxonomy: Calydorea venezolensis is classified within the family Iridaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the iris family. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, such as its leaf and flower structure, as well as its genetic makeup. Calydorea venezolensis is further classified within the genus Calydorea, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The genus Calydorea is part of the tribe Sisyrinchieae, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their showy flowers and tuberous roots. Overall, Calydorea venezolensis is classified as Calydorea venezolensis (family Iridaceae, genus Calydorea, tribe Sisyrinchieae).

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
